contentSlot: Wednesday — strongest hero video of the week
liveProviders: Curio published analytics and viral-intelligence ledger
unavailableProviders: Meta has no open public-Reels viral-discovery API; Facebook observations require a separate evidence import

candidates.topic: The world's slowest experiment has produced only nine drops in nearly a century
candidates.frameZero: THIS LIQUID TAKES YEARS TO DROP
candidates.payoff: Pitch looks solid, but its viscosity is so high that one drop can take years to form.
candidates.whyNow: A current YouTube Short about the world's slowest experiment is a 14.65x channel outlier with 889,824 views and about 32,849 views per hour. The topic also has unusually strong primary-source documentation and a visible, singular hero object.
candidates.productionRisk: The outlier validates demand, not reuse rights. Build from University of Queensland facts and permission-qualified imagery; do not download or imitate the outlier's footage.

evidence.label: Pitch Drop Experiment
evidence.metric: Primary factual source and official experiment page

candidates.topic: A collapsing glacier can expose ice that looks electrically blue
candidates.payoff: Dense old ice absorbs red wavelengths and returns more blue light, revealing its age and compression.
candidates.whyNow: A current Instagram Reel built around a glacier calving and revealing deep blue ice is a 20.0x creator outlier. It matches Curio's immediate-anomaly and raw-phenomenon-audio architecture.
candidates.productionRisk: The visual is footage-dependent and the discovered Reel is not a licensed source. Proceed only if a genuine 1080x1920-capable public-domain or licensed calving shot can be secured.

evidence.label: Glacier calving reveals a deep blue core

candidates.topic: A sliced water balloon keeps its shape for an instant after the rubber is gone
candidates.payoff: Inertia keeps the water nearly still for a moment while the torn rubber snaps away.
candidates.whyNow: A current TikTok slow-motion experiment montage is a 920x creator outlier. Its opening water-balloon slice supplies a strong format signal: the impossible state is fully visible before explanation.
candidates.productionRisk: The discovered montage is reference evidence only. Original high-speed capture is expensive; production needs licensed high-speed footage or a verified reusable source before approval.

candidates.topic: Boaters found a raccoon swimming in the middle of a Texas lake
candidates.frameZero: THIS RACCOON IS MILES FROM SHORE
candidates.payoff: The rescue is emotionally clear, but the story needs a verified distance and outcome before it has a transferable factual ending.
candidates.whyNow: A current local-news YouTube Short is a 545.34x channel outlier with 883,303 views and about 11,521 views per hour. It is strong demand evidence for a compact animal-rescue arc.
candidates.productionRisk: Hold until the original report, exact location, distance, outcome, and footage rights are verified. Virality alone cannot fill factual gaps.

evidence.label: Heartwarming Raccoon Rescue on a Texas Lake
